lede: A look at July 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Amiah Miller and Giuseppe Di Masi, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Mumbai
events.desc: A 100-year-old building in Mumbai, India, collapses, killing at least 10 people and leaving many others trapped.

events.title: 2007 Chūetsu offshore earthquake
events.desc: An earthquake of magnitude 6.8 and 6.6 aftershock occurs off the Niigata coast of Japan killing eight people, injuring at least 800 and damaging a nuclear power plant.

events.title: Millennium Park
events.desc: Millennium Park, considered Chicago's first and most ambitious early 21st-century architectural project, is opened to the public by Mayor Richard M. Daley.

events.title: Comet Shoemaker–Levy 9
events.desc: The comet Shoemaker-Levy 9 is destroyed in a head-on collision with Jupiter.

events.title: Army of the Republic of Vietnam
events.desc: South Vietnamese Colonel Phạm Ngọc Thảo, a formerly undetected communist spy and double agent, is hunted down and killed by unknown individuals after being sentenced to death in absentia for a February 1965 coup attempt against Nguyễn Khánh.

events.title: J. D. Salinger
events.desc: J. D. Salinger publishes his popular yet controversial novel, The Catcher in the Rye.

events.title: Uruguay national football team
events.desc: Uruguay beats hosts Brazil 2–1 to win the World Cup in a match dubbed as the Maracanazo.

events.title: Max Reger
events.desc: Max Reger's Hebbel Requiem is first performed in a memorial concert for the composer, conducted by Philipp Wolfrum.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: At the order of President Abraham Lincoln, Union troops begin a 25-mile march into Virginia for what will become the First Battle of Bull Run, the first major land battle of the war.

events.title: Richard II of England
events.desc: King Richard II of England is crowned.

births.name: Dhanraj Pillay
births.line: Indian field hockey player and manager

births.name: Lido Vieri
births.line: Italian football manager and football player

births.name: Bert Rechichar
births.line: American football defensive back and kicker (died 2019)

births.name: Paul Farnes
births.line: British Royal Air Force (RAF) fighter pilot (died 2020)

deaths.text: Heinz Zemanek, Austrian computer scientist and academic
George Busbee, American lawyer and politician, 77th Governor of Georgia
Johann David Heinichen, German composer and theorist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Our Lady of Mount Carmel Fiesta de La Tirana (Tarapacá Region, Chile)
